headline: How Ukrainians are turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
subhead: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.

headline: US and Iran trade strikes over Strait of Hormuz
Le Pen cuts short first presidential campaign trip amid protests
Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for US Senate, exits race after rape allegation
subhead: Platner, the Democratic Senate nominee for Maine and the party's hope to flip a Republican-held seat, ended his campaign following a rape allegation. He has denied the accusations.

headline: EWC 2026 in Paris: Behind the scenes of the controversial partnership between France and Saudi Arabia in esports
subhead: By hosting this Saudi video game tournament in Paris, with an opening ceremony on Wednesday, July 8, featuring a concert by Aya Nakamura, the Elysée is endorsing the kingdom's dominance in the sector – but not without facing criticism from the French esports community.

headline: Kylian Mbappé: A leader on a mission, both on the pitch and behind the scenes at the 2026 World Cup
subhead: The captain of the France team, who will face Morocco on Thursday, July 9, in the quarterfinals, keeps scoring goals while playing an active role behind the scenes for Les Bleus. His selflessness stands in stark contrast to the criticisms of his supposed individualism.

headline: 'A World Cup is not a reflection of international relations, or if it is, it is a particularly distorted one'
subhead: Aside from the ban imposed on Russia by European and international football authorities after its invasion of Ukraine in 2022, the geopolitics of football has little to do with geopolitics in general, even though the tournament offers plenty of lessons, writesLe Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

subhead: Marine Le Pen announced she would run for president hours after her embezzlement conviction was upheld.

headline: Book reveals Trump's improvisation and concentration of power
subhead: Two journalists from the 'New York Times' retrace the early months of the US president's second term, highlighting the ruptures and upheavals his approach to power has unleashed.

headline: Albert Camus archives, including manuscripts, forged documents and letters, join national collection
subhead: The archives, previously kept within the writer's family, has been acquired by France's national library for €9 million with support from Hermès and CIC.
